decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 family support vs. Edge stability fixes
decisions.context: Multiple family/multi-alignment features in flight (ENG-6231, ENG-7789, ENG-6405, ENG-7858) while Edge has critical stability issues (ENG-9436: lock contention causing exits). Edge is strategic moat builder, but Explore V2 drives north star metric.

arguments.for: Edge stability directly impacts enterprise trust and 'Build the Moat' pillar. Lock contention exits are unacceptable for production deployments. Should pause E2 features to fix foundation.
arguments.against: Family support is core to 'Nail the Core' - enables decisions on fleet-wide patterns. Already 4+ tickets in flight, momentum exists. Edge GUI (ENG-9883) shows we're investing there too. Can parallelize.

decisions.title: Commit to GovCloud/FedRAMP GA date or deprioritize
decisions.context: Multiple GovCloud efforts (ENG-10156 AWS workspace, ENG-9939 Windmill deployment, ENG-9846 FIPS compliance) but no clear ship date. Significant eng investment without visible customer pipeline.
decisions.blockedBy: Customer pipeline clarity - do we have LOIs/contracts justifying continued investment?

arguments.for: GovCloud is strategic moat - competitors can't easily follow. FIPS/FedRAMP opens aerospace/defense verticals. Infrastructure work (ENG-10156, ENG-9939) suggests real customer demand.
arguments.against: Massive distraction from core product. Creates parallel infrastructure to maintain. No evidence this drives north star metric (decisions/customer). Should validate demand before building.

decisions.title: Resolve shadow comparison false negatives before DLv2 rollout
decisions.context: ENG-10117 shows dedup asymmetry causing false negatives in validation. This undermines trust in migration to new data layer - core 'Nail the Core' risk.

arguments.for: Cannot ship DLv2 with known correctness issues. False negatives mean customers miss events/decisions. Breaks trust. Must fix before broader rollout.
arguments.against: Shadow mode exists to catch these issues. Can document known discrepancies and proceed if impact is minimal. Perfect migration is impossible.

decisions.title: Consolidate or kill Canvas given Explore V2 investment
decisions.context: Heavy Explore V2 feature development (families, multi-alignment, axes dropzones ENG-10007, global nav ENG-10084) while Canvas exists as separate product. Strategy lists both but unclear differentiation.

arguments.for: Canvas enables different use case (collaboration/presentation vs analysis). Both serve 'Decisions Captured' north star differently. Keep both, clarify positioning.
arguments.against: Splitting eng resources across two visualization products dilutes 'Nail the Core'. Customers confused by two tools. Merge best of Canvas into Explore V2, sunset Canvas.

decisions.title: Set load testing success criteria beyond pts/sec
decisions.context: ENG-10037 and ENG-10150 target 5M pts/sec but unclear how this maps to customer value or 'Embed Deeper' pillar. Is this the right proxy metric?

arguments.for: Need objective performance benchmarks. 5M pts/sec supports larger deployments, enabling bigger customers (embed deeper). Clear eng goal.
arguments.against: Pts/sec is infrastructure vanity metric. Should measure: queries/decisions per session, time-to-insight, or data scale where customers hit UX degradation. Optimize for decision velocity not throughput.

keyResults.current: 4+ tickets in flight, FE refactor underway

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy backend infrastructure work (data-batcher, DLv2, load testing) competing with frontend feature delivery (E2 families, UX polish). GovCloud creating parallel workstream.
alerts: No visible north star metric instrumentation in eng tickets - are we measuring decisions captured?
5 open decisions require exec alignment by 4/18 - risk of thrash without clarity
Edge stability (ENG-9436) is P0 but not seeing urgency in signals - production risk
Ask SIFT has zero visible progress despite being named strategic product
